title: Castling
summary: Move king and rook in a single step

steps.title: What Is Castling?
steps.text: Castling is the only move in chess where TWO pieces move at once: your king and one rook. It's the fastest way to tuck the king into safety AND activate a rook. You can castle kingside (short) or queenside (long).

steps.title: The Rules
steps.text: To castle: (1) neither the king nor that rook has moved, (2) no pieces are between them, (3) the king is not in check, and (4) the king doesn't pass through or land on an attacked square.

steps.hint: In most chess interfaces you just move the king two squares toward the rook.
Move the king two squares toward the a-file rook.

steps.title: Done!
steps.text: Both kings are safe and both a-file rooks are connected with the other rook. Castling early is one of the most important habits in chess — aim to do it by move 10 in most games.
